sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Right heel pain following triathlon training.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports right heel pain that began after a triathlon training session, typically after workouts or upon waking in the morning. The pain is located in the back of the heel and extends into the calf. He describes the pain as tight and almost tearing upon initial movement, which subsides after approximately 15 minutes of walking. Pain is exacerbated by prolonged sitting followed by standing and also after periods of activity. He reports no recent trauma and does not stretch.
Findings
Physical examination reveals tenderness to palpation of the right heel and calf. Dorsiflexion of the foot elicits pain. Thompson's test is negative. Neurological examination is intact. Inflammatory markers are not yet available.
Assessment
Achilles tendinitis, likely secondary to overuse and inadequate stretching.
Plan
Prescribe ibuprofen 400mg every 6 hours as needed for pain and inflammation. Order physical therapy evaluation and initiation of a stretching and strengthening program. Schedule a follow-up appointment in 7-10 days to assess response to treatment. Advise patient to modify training volume and intensity until symptoms improve.
